mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in months with PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production above 1% signals strengthening manufacturing activity, boosting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in months with INDPRO > 1% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in CPI and rise in housing starts signals stable inflation and robust consumer demand, creating a favorable environment for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with CPI < 235 and HOUST > 1000 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the federal funds rate above 2% signals tightening monetary policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in months with FEDFUNDS > 2% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP growth below 2% signals economic contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in quarters with GDPC1 < 2% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 7% signals labor market weakness, reducing consumer spending and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in months with UNRATE > 7% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 98.5 signals weakening manufacturing activity, reducing corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in months with INDPRO < 98.5 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.
